NatFunTableExamples.solution:
Module NatFunTableExamples.
  Module NatVal.
    Definition V := nat.
    Definition default := 0.
  End NatVal.
  Module NatFunTable := FunTable NatVal.
  Import NatFunTable.
  Example ex1 : get 0 empty = 0.
  Proof. reflexivity. Qed.
  Example ex2 : get 0 (set 0 42 empty) = 42.
  Proof. reflexivity. Qed.
  Example ex3 : get 1 (set 0 42 empty) = 0.
  Proof. reflexivity. Qed.
End NatFunTableExamples.
NatFunTableExamples.explanation: Define a NatVal module with V=nat and default=0, instantiate FunTable, and write unit tests.

lists_table.solution:
Module ListsTable (VT : ValType) <: Table.
  Definition V := VT.V.
  Definition default := VT.default.
  Definition key := nat.
  Definition table := list (key * V).
  Definition empty : table := [].
  Fixpoint get (k : key) (t : table) : V :=
    match t with
    | [] => default
    | (k', v) :: t' => if k =? k' then v else get k t'
    end.
  Definition set (k : key) (v : V) (t : table) : table := (k, v) :: t.
  Theorem get_empty_default: forall (k : key), get k empty = default.
  Proof. intros. reflexivity. Qed.
  Theorem get_set_same: forall (k : key) (v : V) (t : table), get k (set k v t) = v.
  Proof. intros. simpl. bdestruct (k =? k). reflexivity. lia. Qed.
  Theorem get_set_other: forall (k k' : key) (v : V) (t : table), k <> k' -> get k' (set k v t) = get k' t.
  Proof. intros. simpl. bdestruct (k' =? k). lia. reflexivity. Qed.
End ListsTable.

(* Examples: *)
Module StringListsTableExamples.
  Module StringListsTable := ListsTable StringVal.
  Import StringListsTable.
  Open Scope string_scope.
  Example ex1 : get 0 empty = "". Proof. reflexivity. Qed.
  Example ex2 : get 0 (set 0 "A" empty) = "A". Proof. reflexivity. Qed.
  Example ex3 : get 1 (set 0 "A" empty) = "". Proof. reflexivity. Qed.
End StringListsTableExamples.
lists_table.explanation: Implement Table using association lists. get scans the list for the key. set prepends a new binding. Proofs use bdestruct.

list_etable_abs.solution:
Module ListETableAbs (VT : ValType) <: ETableAbs.
  Definition V := VT.V.
  Definition default := VT.default.
  Definition key := nat.
  Definition table := list (key * V).
  Definition empty : table := [].
  Fixpoint get (k : key) (t : table) : V :=
    match t with
    | [] => default
    | (k', v) :: t' => if k =? k' then v else get k t'
    end.
  Definition set (k : key) (v : V) (t : table) : table := (k, v) :: t.
  Fixpoint bound (k : key) (t : table) : bool :=
    match t with
    | [] => false
    | (k', _) :: t' => if k =? k' then true else bound k t'
    end.
  Definition elements (t : table) : list (key * V) := t.
  Definition Abs (t : table) : partial_map V := map_of_list t.
  Definition rep_ok (t : table) : Prop := True.
  Theorem empty_ok : rep_ok empty. Proof. constructor. Qed.
  Theorem set_ok : forall (k : key) (v : V) (t : table), rep_ok t -> rep_ok (set k v t). Proof. intros. constructor. Qed.
  Theorem empty_relate : Abs empty = empty_map. Proof. reflexivity. Qed.
  Theorem bound_relate : forall (t : table) (k : key), rep_ok t -> map_bound k (Abs t) = bound k t.
  Proof.
    intros t k _. unfold Abs, map_bound, bound. induction t as [| [k' v'] t' IH].
    - simpl. reflexivity.
    - simpl. bdestruct (k =? k').
      + subst. rewrite update_eq. simpl. reflexivity.
      + rewrite update_neq by lia. apply IH.
  Qed.
  Theorem lookup_relate : forall (t : table) (k : key), rep_ok t -> map_find default k (Abs t) = get k t.
  Proof.
    intros t k _. unfold Abs, map_find, find, get. induction t as [| [k' v'] t' IH].
    - simpl. unfold empty, t_empty. reflexivity.
    - simpl. bdestruct (k =? k').
      + subst. rewrite update_eq. reflexivity.
      + rewrite update_neq by lia. apply IH.
  Qed.
  Theorem insert_relate : forall (t : table) (k : key) (v : V), rep_ok t -> map_update k v (Abs t) = Abs (set k v t). Proof. intros. reflexivity. Qed.
  Theorem elements_relate : forall (t : table), rep_ok t -> Abs t = map_of_list (elements t). Proof. intros. reflexivity. Qed.
End ListETableAbs.
list_etable_abs.explanation: Implement ETableAbs with association lists. rep_ok is trivially True. Abs is map_of_list. Some relate proofs require more work.

list_queue.solution:
Module ListQueue <: Queue.
  Definition V := nat.
  Definition queue := list V.
  Definition empty : queue := [].
  Definition is_empty (q : queue) : bool := match q with [] => true | _ => false end.
  Definition enq (q : queue) (v : V) : queue := q ++ [v].
  Definition deq (q : queue) : queue := match q with [] => [] | _ :: t => t end.
  Definition peek (default : V) (q : queue) : V := match q with [] => default | h :: _ => h end.
  Theorem is_empty_empty : is_empty empty = true. Proof. reflexivity. Qed.
  Theorem is_empty_nonempty : forall q v, is_empty (enq q v) = false.
  Proof. intros. unfold enq. destruct q; reflexivity. Qed.
  Theorem peek_empty : forall d, peek d empty = d. Proof. reflexivity. Qed.
  Theorem peek_nonempty : forall d q v, peek d (enq q v) = peek v q.
  Proof. intros. unfold enq, peek. destruct q; reflexivity. Qed.
  Theorem deq_empty : deq empty = empty. Proof. reflexivity. Qed.
  Theorem deq_nonempty : forall q v, deq (enq q v) = if is_empty q then q else enq (deq q) v.
  Proof. intros. unfold enq, deq, is_empty. destruct q; simpl; reflexivity. Qed.
End ListQueue.
list_queue.explanation: Implement Queue using lists where enq appends to the end (linear time). deq and peek operate on the front.

two_list_queue.solution:
Theorem empty_relate : Abs empty = []. Proof. reflexivity. Qed.
Theorem enq_relate : forall q v, Abs (enq q v) = (Abs q) ++ [v].
Proof. intros [f b] v. unfold Abs, enq. simpl. rewrite app_assoc. reflexivity. Qed.
Theorem peek_relate : forall d q, peek d q = hd d (Abs q).
Proof. intros d [f b]. unfold Abs, peek. destruct f.
  - simpl. destruct (rev b); reflexivity.
  - simpl. reflexivity. Qed.
Theorem deq_relate : forall q, Abs (deq q) = tl (Abs q).
Proof. intros [f b]. unfold Abs, deq. destruct f.
  - simpl. destruct (rev b) eqn:E; simpl; try reflexivity.
    rewrite app_nil_r. reflexivity.
  - simpl. reflexivity. Qed.
two_list_queue.explanation: Verify the two-list queue implementation. Each proof unfolds definitions and reasons about the front and back lists.

a_vector.explanation: Construct a vector with list [1;2;3] and length 3, proving length [1;2;3] = 3 by reflexivity.

vector_cons_correct.solution:
Definition vector_cons {X : Type} (x : X) (v : vector X) : vector X.
Proof.
  destruct v as [[xs n] Hlen].
  apply (exist _ (x :: xs, S n)).
  simpl. rewrite Hlen. reflexivity.
Defined.

Theorem vector_cons_correct : forall (X : Type) (x : X) (v : vector X),
    list_of_vector (vector_cons x v) = x :: (list_of_vector v).
Proof.
  intros. destruct v as [[xs n] Hlen]. simpl. reflexivity.
Qed.
vector_cons_correct.explanation: vector_cons prepends x to the list and increments the length. Correctness follows by destructing the vector.

vector_app_correct.solution:
Definition vector_app {X : Type} (v1 v2 : vector X) : vector X.
Proof.
  destruct v1 as [[xs1 n1] H1].
  destruct v2 as [[xs2 n2] H2].
  apply (exist _ (xs1 ++ xs2, n1 + n2)).
  simpl. rewrite app_length. lia.
Defined.

Theorem vector_app_correct : forall (X : Type) (v1 v2 : vector X),
    list_of_vector (vector_app v1 v2) = list_of_vector v1 ++ list_of_vector v2.
Proof.
  intros. destruct v1 as [[xs1 n1] H1]. destruct v2 as [[xs2 n2] H2].
  simpl. reflexivity.
Qed.
vector_app_correct.explanation: vector_app concatenates the lists and adds the lengths. Correctness is immediate after destructing.
